task_completion.description: Docker Desktop or a terminal window is open to interact with Docker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window with a command prompt or the Docker Desktop application interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the pull request for the specific python image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command 'docker pull python:3.11-slim' is typed in the terminal, or 'python:3.11-slim' is entered into the search/pull bar within Docker Desktop.

task_completion.description: The image layers are being downloaded from Docker Hub.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'Pulling from library/python' with downloading progress bars/hashes, or a downloading status indicator in Docker Desktop.

task_completion.description: The Images section in Docker Desktop is opened to verify availability.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Docker Desktop GUI is open and the 'Images' tab (specifically 'Local' images) is selected and active.

task_completion.description: The python:3.11-slim image is successfully listed as available in Docker Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Docker Desktop Images list, there is an entry with the repository 'python' (or 'python:3.11-slim') and the tag '3.11-slim', showing it is locally available.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Docker Desktop application or a real terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shows standard OS terminal chrome or the official Docker Desktop UI, rather than a text editor or mock mockup.

integrity.description: The image was actually pulled from Docker Hub, not just simulated or renamed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for authentic layer download progress (multiple layer IDs downloading/extracting) rather than just a 'docker tag' command renaming an existing image.

integrity.description: The image entry in Docker Desktop appears as a genuine software element.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image entry in Docker Desktop includes standard metadata like Image Size, Created date, and action buttons (Run, Delete), proving it is a real loaded image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is an official Docker Hub image. The expected repository name is 'python', the expected tag is '3.11-slim', and the full image name is 'python:3.11-slim'. Verify that this exact repository and tag combination appears in the Docker Desktop local images list.
